name: Octavia Butler's Lauren Olamina
creator: Octavia Butler
practice: Community building in collapse, hyperempathy as design constraint, founding Earthseed
approach: When existing communities collapse, build new ones from whoever survives. Hyperempathy — literally feeling others' pain — as the foundation for community design. The only lasting truth is Change. Shape it or be destroyed by it.
body_of_work: Lauren Olamina navigates a collapsed California (2024-2027), building Acorn community from refugees, ex-slaves, and wanderers. Earthseed's principles: God is Change, shape Change or be shaped. Creates community through radical inclusion under shared purpose.
why_instructive: She builds a dome from scratch — not through systems but through shared philosophy. Her hyperempathy is the design constraint: you CANNOT ignore suffering because you literally feel it. This is what person-centered means.

dome_relevance.4: Hyperempathy as health condition that shapes all interactions
dome_relevance.10: Autonomy — choosing to lead, choosing to build
dome_relevance.12: Flourishing defined as purposeful adaptation to change

sphere_relevance.5: Social fabric woven from strangers through shared purpose
sphere_relevance.6: Temporal — building from the ruins of what was
sphere_relevance.7: Activation — Acorn as intentional community activation
sphere_relevance.8: Permanence — what lasts when everything else collapses
